description: Create a GitLab CI pipeline that uses the node:4 Docker image with a docker tag runner, defines three stages (generate, build, publish) and caches the path gen/node_modules/. In the generate stage add a generate job that runs on the java:8 image and executes the shell commands: prerelease="-ci.$CI_BUILD_REF"; if [ $CI_BUILD_REF_NAME = "develop" ]; then prerelease="-dev.$CI_BUILD_ID"; fi; if [ -n "$CI_BUILD_TAG" ]; then prerelease=''; fi; sed -i -r "s/\"npmVersion\": \"([0-9\.]+)-local\",/\"npmVersion\": \"\1$prerelease\",/" config.json; if grep -q -- "-local" config.json; then echo "Version wasn't changed"; exit 1; fi; ./scripts/generate.sh; and stores the gen directory as an artifact for 1 hour. In the build stage add a build:pack job that depends on generate and runs the commands: npm config set unsafe-perm true; mkdir build; node ./scripts/update-generated-code.js; cd gen; npm install; npm pack; cp *.tgz ../build/; and archives the build directory as an artifact for 1 day. In the publish stage add a publish:npm-dev job that depends on build:pack, runs only on the develop branch of janslow/gitlab-swagger-client, sets the variable tag to dev, writes the npm auth token to ~/.npmrc with echo "//registry.npmjs.org/:_authToken=$NPM_TOKEN" > ~/.npmrc, then runs npm publish --ignore-scripts --tag $tag ./build/*.tgz to the npm registry in the dev environment. Also add a publish:npm-latest job that runs only on tags, sets tag to latest, uses the same script to publish to npm in the latest environment.
